channel.title: Blogs on Script Adults
channel.description: Recent content in Blogs on Script Adults

item.title: Even a chinchilla is better than you in language learning
item.description:
Ok it is definitely a clickbait. But as a native speaker of a language (Cantonese, Hong Kong/GuangZhou dialect) that does not have voicing constracts in its consonants, I had a real hard time in differentiate voiced and voiceless consonants when I started learning Japanese (which has the voicing constracts, and the voiceless consonants are unaspirated in native speakers’ mouths most of the time).

It was >10 years ago and I still remember how many times I listened and pronounced voiced and voiceless consonants with a Praat installed in my computer, and looked at the spectrograms allllll day long.

item.title: 幼貧而樂今殷而戚問何所為也
item.description:
坐立皆不適，忡忡何所憂？
居毋患寒暑，食盡啖珍饈。
幼自貧窮慣，今唯前咎愁。
何如曲肱枕，淡飯敢焚裘。

item.title: 隱學典難覓
item.description:
情鍾隱學典難覓，眼澀抬頭日已斜。
古籍奇書尚能借，春秋有數問誰賒。

item.title: 自樂
item.description:
懶理職場獻媚密，歸來自聽雨連珠。
凡生笑我無心眼，漆吏知吾乃樂魚。
孔聖隨心不踰矩，詩痴久諷律無虞。
唯耽無益雕蟲技，最是優悠意氣舒。

item.title: The Courier and the truck
item.description:
The Courier and the truck; complement and substitute

Although people say “this time is different”, there is something in common.

In the ancient ancient times, if you needed a parcel moved across the country, you hired a body. The good courier had good strength to carry the parcels, strong legs, deep lungs, and a tolerance for going without (too much) rest. Those were the metrics. Speed was a property of the person, so you paid for the person who had the most of it.

item.title: Understanding Yoneda lemma
item.description:
The Yoneda Lemma

The Yoneda lemma has been being complainted as “hard to understand” for a long time. Indeed it is not very easy, so I was trying to come up with a way to understand it more intuitively.

Everyone knows the analogy by Ravi Vakil:

You work at a particle accelerator. You want to understand some particle. All you can do are throw other particles at it and see what happens. If you understand how your mystery particle responds to all possible test particles at all possible test energies, then you know everything there is to know about your mystery particle.

item.title: 文采狂
item.description:
分明昨日痾甚篤，費解今宵文采狂。
病體疑為煉丹鼎，丹成病好筆生光。

item.title: 論文化侵蝕
item.description:
近世多鵲壘，鳲鳩豐尾垂。
鳲鳩施小利，鵲語改相隨。
客佔雖多地，東家尚有墟。
毋蹈楚王轍，魯莽贈烏騅。

item.title: 不寐又思早起
item.description:
寅時初夏雨，淅瀝擾休眠。
冷氣頭上響，炎風廳裡燃。
閒姑倚書案，久若坐針氈。
旦日還待呷，齋啡微苦漣。

item.title: 憶杯渡
item.description:
童年夏必逢飛蟻，舍妹心驚急淚潺。
晚煮杯茶憶杯渡，青絲初白夢青山。

item.title: 贈妻一絕
item.description:
昏燈暖透夜閨房，細看床前換寢裝。
枉詡能詩多健筆，無言倚枕醉幽芳。

item.title: 問道
item.description:
行年三十一，佇路覓繩韁。
問道莊周樹，尋花蝶夢鄉。

item.title: 職場自嘲
item.description:
力壯常揮劍，堂堂六呎夫。
惶惶乞升斗，瘁死有餘辜。

item.title: 鑽石山母親節聚餐
item.description:
蓮園喜無雨，好節聚茶几。
醉焗竹蟶嫩，香煎露筍肥。
親應少時養，食慾老來微。
尚慰纖纖箸，頻頻碟上飛。

item.title: 易主
item.description:
老號金主易，才賢歸梓桑。
坊中無事瑣，役婦手機忙。
既屢裁員狠，還期仕氣昂？
時衰沐猴冠，豎子坐高堂。

item.title: 憶中大
item.description:
夢入當年新亞宿，涼乘合一水旁陰。
青山一別書香薄，石壁空鐫名姓深。
錯把孤居作勤學，徒懷熱鬧論經音。
盈頭鬒髮今朝落，默撫長鬚復擁衾。

item.title: 詠巴西柔術
item.description:
褒衣博帶非儒服，肉搏毫釐不動刀。
灑汗披襟相浹背，騎胸抱頸若分桃。
難如對奕能開智，巧使剛柔解六韜。
此藝修心養吾性，藏鋒斂戾伏波濤。

item.title: 喜見群友良緣
item.description:
喜見同群締佳偶，如聞弄玉鳳凰簫。
誰云嗜語招人異，得遇才媛桃自夭。

item.title: 歸人
item.description:
滾滾車連轂，歸人溢路邊。
霓虹遮月影，路樹攝塵煙。
俗務何竭慮，三旬無撫弦。
樓高山不語，夜靜反無眠。

item.title: 大涌橋路
item.description:
向晚歸車涌上過，遺田古早有牛鞅。
朝擠港島珠纓路，夜食沙田布袋糧。
幾覆瀝青橋主易，唯留兩岸水湄長。
幽村狗喚誰家鬼，一叫三迴促斷腸。

item.title: 閱杜甫生平
item.description:
嘗題蜀相三分國，又寄宮垣想玉珂。
久憶洛都疲慧眼，心傷楚地殞冬河。
憂民睿敏蟬聯出，不變江山又幾多。
宋滅元亡明復繼，何需俊傑枉蹉跎。

item.title: 望華街
item.description:
日出華街盈客旅，深宵穢巷舞蠅蚊。
光陰不惜花齡女，玉手磨成半片皸。
昨夢金貂英氣煥，明朝滿鏡老眉矄。
三坪礎剷千年樹，百載勞來兩尺墳。

item.title: Work from home
item.description:
職務能遙理，誰踰廨署埤。
螢屏傳案牘，午灶暖餘糜。
滌釜甘霖躍，臨窗鬧巷熙。
同僚誠喜見，更愛自家籬。

item.title: 古不賤今
item.description:
人常詠古歌仁聖，似是今人鄙不勝。
叔夜孤高難仿效，夷齊朽滯若瘕癥。
西山槁槁饑薇蕨，一曲淒淒絕廣陵。
史內千秋寸心辨，賢愚莫有固規繩。

item.title: 見族人炫祖上之富
item.description:
誰家三水田千里，舊榭新軒盡姓陳。
輒耀膏粱矜祖富，詩書少讀曝詞貧。
詢其祖業餘多少，賞彼紅腮半怯嗔。
渴鬼愁看舊時屋，年年祭饗供他人。

item.title: 無題
item.description:
陳衣欲搗子規啼，嗽穿重簾夜更淒。
十載茅齋茂陵臥，雙行枯淚太常妻。
清心昔捨青雲路，俗務今催洗耳溪。
半世徒勞芳自賞，來生捨筆執鋤犁。

item.title: 憶遊摩周湖
item.description:
久慕摩周泊，霙遊弟子町。
盤湖青壁峭，漫靄雪濱暝。
水闊連雲影，藍深莫片萍。
天鵝神渚過，幾欲問仙齡。

item.title: 漫郎愁
item.description:
平生福禍求諸己，豈冀張良借箸籌。
怨得春時秋亦去，芳時不待漫郎愁。

item.title: 拗相公
item.description:
煩言少語漠修飾，鐵石心腸拗相公。
滿腹嗔痴熬鬱結，唯題恚賦怨長空。

item.title: 春病
item.description:
春衫濕濕雨還淹，爽颯何知昨日炎。
一暖一寒多感冒，鉛頭鉛瞼鐵毫尖。

item.title: 宿酒
item.description:
乃耳春桃赤，方乾竟欲斟。
酣歌忘篋笥，宿酒爛柯尋。

item.title: 直鉤詞
item.description:
嘗聞我氏姜牙祖，百煉參來妙直鉤。
際遇諒如垂澗釣，樵漁宿志待成周。

item.title: 望金鐘雨
item.description:
眺望金鐘雨，禁思海外朋。
聞琴忘荈冷，曲散熄孤燈。

item.title: 白梣詠
item.description:
郊車瞥白梣，逸氣惹馳心。
獨直杆齊宇，無憂眾鑠金。
貧泥生色潔，倔質抵風侵。
且倚柴亭樹，朱門少此陰。

item.title: 苦戀樹
item.description:
苦楝鄉音訛苦戀，人皆笑我若情痴。
情皆巧合亦如此，插柳方成連理枝。

item.title: 煙塵望
item.description:
稻謝樓空鳥失巢，矛平戟鏽馬生郊。
髫兒道側尋劬母，怕見霜綾掛樹梢。

item.title: 理箱見舊稿
item.description:
嘗來翻舊札，拙作惡難評。
弱歲初經事，繁文欲釣名。
浮詞堆綺句，躁筆抹虛情。
老喜栽詩卉，閒窗細剪莖。

item.title: 晚詠
item.description:
山連千樹翠，暉映半川紅。
日暮同歌賦，杯乾意未窮。

item.title: 茗汁詞
item.description:
學淺無他藝，唯長雕琢詞。
沉吟常入定，點卯每來遲。
久病多清興，離群出詭思。
方酣濡墨盡，茗汁代塗之。

item.title: 宦海
item.description:
棲身宦海辭難直，隱沒丹心色自萎。
若學嵇公棱角出，便難割肉細君遺。

item.title: 閏怨
item.description:
涼風捲髮斷多少，深館籠眉鎖幾重。
縱有沉魚驚世色，夫征不復枉芳容。

item.title: 詠志
item.description:
生似離弦箭，宜將鵠志求。
才情師子建，韜略效留侯。
過隧應斬岸，逾河當折舟。
無教箭虛落，白髮泣春秋。

item.title: 李恥杜愁
item.description:
性俗全無墨在胸，冥搜拙句砌機峰。
詩成引得謫仙恥，筆落愁催工部容。
既與深情詩韻繫，何教秉賦櫟樗庸。
如吾堪作違心事，簡朽帛殘枯筆封。

item.title: 野火
item.description:
野火吹焦來撲鼻，疑斯處亦啟兵端。
蒼天果有好生德，怎使狼煙剪不完。

item.title: 稱病歸沐其二
item.description:
欲借銀鉤掛輕絅，披襟快意抱江潮。
紅塵每每多煩事，擲笏臥花看九霄。

item.title: 灣仔商廈觀圬者
item.description:
久困雕欄內，而無玉砌求。
無乾圬者汗，盡濕富人樓。
貴族連三代，貧門到白頭。
笙歌聽愈擾，喋喋不安休。

item.title: 遙寄故人
item.description:
昨問他鄉花幾許，君稱今日滿山紅。
東風知汝遙憶我，一片吹來孤座中。

item.title: 稱病歸沐登獅子山
item.description:
甫陟蒼巔氣清朗，忽憂仕路暗傷神。
何時始得閒如許，莫待衰顏惹滓塵。

item.title: 夜讀
item.description:
無意尋春色，離群獨筆耕。
風幽星火動，葉蕩夜蟲鳴。
案上半尋闊，心田萬畝宏。
思潮如碧海，引棹到三更。

item.title: 不寐
item.description:
夜起喉酸胸似火，窗前恍惚浴寒暉。
素娥招我隨風去，忘卻愁思入繡闈。

item.title: 敢擬一詩忖度離鄉客家人愁緒
item.description:
久滯繁華地，鄉離語更乖。
夢中歸故里，簷下一聲𠊎。

item.title: 書癖
item.description:
鼯埋松果鴃懸屍，陋室徒藏百冊詩。
開卷恐污長不讀，秦皇應笑我愚痴。

item.title: 葵涌工廈會友
item.description:
取徑葵涌道，良朋相飲來。
人閒車馬靜，路黦綠苔開。
風送天雲遠，霞留暮色哀。
頹垣將拆盡，客別舊樓臺。

item.title: 悼從祖祖父
item.description:
清明未至立新冢，香火盈堂幡引魂。
冒雨梨花開遍野，迎風淚眼念先恩。

item.title: 冀嶺南景色依舊
item.description:
樹倚東籬側，清池阡陌間。
萬田如一色，青嶂繞千鬟。
胡寇鐵馬盛，唐家薪火頑。
北風南嶺絕，不許渡韶關。

item.title: 春分始聞春雷
item.description:
今歲春雷晚，牆陰蘚早成。
雲烏風驟起，一震百禽鳴。

item.title: 回鄉聞兒童皆不能語方言
item.description:
塾盡異文授，童皆方語非。
隔山車馬鬧，鄰舍灶煙微。
子弟傳承懶，田間稼穡稀。
落花歸土去，不復護芳菲。

item.title: The overthinking of LLM
item.description:
I asked Deepseek to draw the Schlegel diagram of a hexahedron — should be a very simple task right? Just 2 squares and link up their vertices. And yet I noticed something interesting: it tends to overthink and end up outputting nonsense answers when DeepThink is on (which is supposed to be more powerful and “clever”), but getting the task done correctly when DeepThink is off. Huh.

So this is the prompt I gave Deepseek:

item.title: Calculating the homology of Poincaré's homology sphere through dodecahedron and cellular homology
item.description:
Homology sphere

A homology sphere is a (closed connected oriented) $n$-mainfolds with the same homology as the $n$-sphere. Poincaré first conjectured that any $n$-manifolds homologous (i.e. having the same homology) to the $n$-sphere should be homeomorphic to the $n$-sphere, then later he found a counterexample, which led him to a modified conjecture (which is the Poincaré’s conjecture).

The counterexample he found is very interesting, one of the way to construct it is the folowing:

item.title: The H+ doner and acceptor analogy in universal coefficient theorem for homology
item.description:
Using fields to detect integral homology?

So in the universal coefficient theorem, by taking $R = \mathbb{Z}$, we know that there is a short exact sequence (which splits):

$0 \rightarrow H_n(C_{*}) \otimes_R M \rightarrow H_n(C_{*} \otimes_R M) \rightarrow Tor^R_1(H_{n-1}(C_*), M) \rightarrow 0$

Some interesting cases are when $M = \mathbb{Z}_{p} \text{ or } \mathbb{Q}$ , do the homology over $\mathbb{Q}$ and $\mathbb{Z}_p$ tells us something about the integral homology? In particular, if $\tilde{H}(X; \mathbb{Q}) = 0$ and $\tilde{H}(X; \mathbb{Z}_p) = 0$ for all $p$, does it imply that $\tilde{H}(X) = 0$ ? .This is a problem suggested in [Kup2020]1.

item.title: Most hilarious exercise I have seen so far
item.description: That’s why I love Arnold’s books.

item.title: The double dual trick to construct a better object from the existing one
item.description:
Stone–Čech compactification

One of the proof of the Stone–Čech compactification is to consider the continuous functions from a given topological space $X$ to $[0, 1]$.

Crux 1: constructing $[0, 1]^{C}$

Let $C$ be the space of all continuous functions from $X$ to $[0, 1]$, consider $[0, 1]^{C}$, there is a natural map from $X$ to $[0, 1]^{C}$: for each $x \in X$, define $\phi(x) = f \mapsto f(x)$. With product topology $\phi$ is continuous. Also by Tychonnof’s theorem $[0, 1]^{C}$ is compact. Now the closure of the image is obviously compact Hausdorff, and it satisfies a nice property: any continuous map $f : X \to [0, 1]$ extends uniquely to a continuous map $\overline{\phi(X)} \to [0,1]$: for any $\phi(x)$, it has to map to $f(x)$!

item.title: Playful observations with deformation retracts and path connected-ness
item.description:
I was going through a problem in Lee’s Topological Manifolds book1 (problem 7-12). It states that the infinite broom has a strong deformation retract to $(0, 0)$ but not for $(1, 0)$ (only a deformation retract).

Below is some of my playful observations inspired by this problem. (Not restricted to the infinite broom space.)

Inifinite Broom

Note that the deformation retract to $(0, 0)$ itselfs imply the deformation retract to $(1, 0)$, since one can define the deformation retract to $(1, 0)$ by “first retract to $(0, 0)$, then push it to the point $(1, 0)$ along the line joining these two points”.

item.title: Amazed by how ring theory can be linked up to topology
item.description:
I am reviewing some fundamental algebra, and I just learnt something beautifully suggesting the connection of ring theory and geometry, which made me eager to learn some algebraic geometry (which I was not very interested when I was in undergrad). It is an exercise in Aluffi’s book1.

Lets’ jump into it.

The problem

Let $K$ be a compact topological space, and let $R$ be the ring of continuous real-valued functions on $K$, with addition and multiplication defined pointwise.
(i) For $p \in K$, let $M_p = \{f \in R | f(p) = 0\}$. Prove that $M_p$ is a maximal ideal in $R$.
(ii) Prove that if $f_1, \dots , f_r \in R$ have no common zeros, then $(f1, \dots , fr)$ = $(1)$.
(Hint: Consider $f^2_1 + \dots + f^2_r$ .)
(iii) Prove that every maximal ideal $M$ in $R$ is of the form $M_p$ for some $p \in K$.
(Hint: You will use the compactness of $K$ and (ii).)

item.title: The true talent is the ability to carry on
item.description:
Long time ago I read a quote from Habu1(as shown in the screenshot):

The true talent is the ability to carry on with passion, even when it probably won’t pay off.

(not translated word by word)

I was a teenager and it did not make any sense to me. How could passion be the true talent? Look at those geniuses like Gauss, Mozart, John von Neumann! What they have done is not something that could have been done by normal people. True talent is what they have that made them do the amazing things, I thought.

item.title: A more intuitive explanation of Burnside's lemma
item.description: Warning

item.title: An elementary solution of a weird intergal problem (Putnam 1985 A5)
item.description:
Problem

Let $I_{m} = \int^{2\pi}_{0} \cos(x)\cos(2x)\dots\cos(mx) dx$. For $m$ in $1, 2, \dots, 10$, for which $m$ is $I_m \neq 0$ ?

Solution

$I_m$ is non-zero if and only if $m \equiv 0, 3 \pmod{4}$.

Main idea

The official(seemingly? It is in the putnam problem book and a few solutions I found online do the same.) solution is to substitute $\cos x = \frac{e^{ix} + e^{-ix}}{2}$ followed by grouping the terms into $\cos x \cos (2x) \dots \cos (mx) = e^{\text{something}}$, and analyze the something. This approach of cause is of cause good in its own right: it is exactly how you split up the $e^{in\theta}$ terms into triginometric polynomials (or ) in Fourier analysis 101. However it is a little bit annoying to manipulate those powers of $e$. And for this problem it could be done much in a much simpler and elementary way: exploiting the point of symmetry.

item.title: [Updated 20250408]RISC-V explainer
item.description: Info

item.title: [Updated 20250331]Centrality: how we actually perceive the severity of a bug
item.description: Info

item.title: Picking grammatical mistakes in an insightful article
item.description:
After the huge attention gained by deepseek R1, I have seen a good amount of people immediately went to looking for censorships (which are expected to exist), laughed at it, and looked down on it. They totally missed the points and these behaviors made them look like slaves of their very own defense mechanism1.

Deepseek did make some techonological breakthroughs. Indeed the censorship is a defect of such a great product. However making jokes on it with its censorships is just like catching grammatical errors in an insightful article and disdain the article because of that. Yes, grammatical errors are errors, catching itself could be useful, but doing that for attacking an insightsul article is another thing. It just misses the point.

item.title: Added tikzjax support
item.description:
Just added support for tikzjax since I received some feedback about my hand-drawn diagrams are hard to read…now I can use nice graphs like these:

source: https://tikzjax.com/

source: https://tikzjax.com/

source: https://tikz.net/

item.title: Our legal (and moral) system flavors the jerks
item.description:
Disclaimer: The author’s knowledge about the legal system by no means applies to anywhere outside of his homeland. But what is legal or illegal should be similar enough for the discussion below - at least for first world countries, I guess.
That said, the author is not a lawyer and does not have accurate knowledge of any concrete laws. But one does not need to know (too many of) them for this article.

item.title: Why use binary search when you can guess where it should be
item.description:
Why ignore 50% of what we know about the data1

Everyone learnt binary search in Algo 101. It is the fastest way (among comparison based searches) to find an element in a sorted array. All you need to carry out the algorithms is the comparison between the target element and the element at the current position. It is widely applicable because it assumes so little from the data. But for many real life problems, we do often know something apart from merely the comparison between the two numbers. For examples:

item.title: Work at the level of abstraction that works for you
item.description:
Ladder of abstraction

Well the title is obviously a tweak of the “ladder of abstraction”. For those who does not know, “ladder of abstraction” refers to the classic essay by Bret Victor. I am kind of stealing this terminilogy for the reader to visualize climbing up and down on the ladder of abstraction to make it easier to explain the nature of different jobs. The main question this blog post deals with is:

item.title: Relaxation is the result of mastery. With thoughts about problem solving
item.description:
Relaxation as a result of mastery, instead of a prerequisites

In many sports/ crafts that I have experienced, relaxation is considered by many the prerequisites of mastery:

  • In playing piano, your fingers, wrists, arms and shoulders have to be relax or you will find it extremely hard to press the right keys fluently;
  • In online games that require fast reactions, rookies hands are stiff and they press buttons with unnecessary strength and range of motion, which stops them from pressing right buttons at the right time smoothly;
  • In jiujitsu, being reasonably relax helps you to perform efficient moves, saving your energy and gives you more choices in different situations in which you have to adapt and react quickly.

Mainstream arguments I have seen in the above fields are that relaxation is a prerequisite of getting good at these skills: “You have to relax or your chords sound horrible!”, “you have to relax to improve your techniques”. Yet most people have to go through a good amount of practice untill they become relaxed and start being good at those skills. Did they not understand how to relax their bodies when they were beginners? Everyone knows how to relax their bodies - just lie on a comfortable massage bed and you will be relax. The hard part is being relax while doing things that we are not good at.

item.title: Reinventing Catalan numbers
item.description:
Counting binary trees

I was thinking about of the problem of balancing a binary tree, and my mind stumbled across to the question “How many different binary trees with labelled nodes can you make without changing the traversal order?”. After figuring out the answer myself I realized that the numbers of such binary trees are just Catalan numebrs (I was not really into combinatorics - now I am).

For example, the following has traversal order 1->2->3->4->5->6->7

item.title: From casual description of Turing Machine to the density of uncomputable functions
item.description:
Defining the machine by describing it

In Sipser’s Introduction to the theory of computation, a alternative way of defining a Turing machine (other than defining the formal 7-tuple, which is a PITA) - by its “description”. Example (from the proof of $A_{TM}$ is deciable):

M = “On input <B, w>, where B is a DFA and w is a string: 1. Simulate B on input w. 2. If the simulation ends in an accept state, accept . If it ends in a nonaccepting state, reject .”

item.title: Why do I hate using chatGPT to replace search engines
item.description:
My pc is overheated how do i cool it down because it is my life1

Someone said that chatGPT will replace Google. Some kids/ early teenagers do often ask chatGPT for things that could have been found by Googling.

I really hate the idea of replacing search engines by chatGPT. The problem I have with it is not really about AI, but about how do we interact with it.

When we were kids and learnt how to use search engines, we acquired the skills of capturing important parts of the questions in our minds and turn them into keywords. Bashing questions like “my hard disk is full how can I get more space” usually get worse results than things like “hard disk space full solutions” as their counterparts.

item.title: Unity Game Reversing(1): Setup
item.description:
Introduction

Recently I am trying to reverse engineering a simple Windows desktop game made with Unity. I took a look on some reference but found that the setup can be a bit frustrating. This post is attempting to make the setup clearer and more followable. Reference links are listed below.

Tools

Our target is to decompile and debug the Assembly-CSharp.dll inside the folder <game root folder>\<GANE_NAME>_Data\Managed\, which contains custom code the game developer wrote, not the code of Unity or other frameworks. It is written in C#. We need the below tools:

item.title: LOTS Project - Paypal
item.description:
Introduction

LOTS project, founded by mrd0x, is a collection of websites which is likely be trusted but can be used to evade detection when conducting phishing, C&C, exfiltration and downloading tools. In this post I will introduce a way to abusing PayPal and hopefully will be contributing to the LOTS project.

This series is (intentively) for my ideas on novel exfiltration/ C&C channels.

Exfiltraftion by Paypal

In Paypal, one can dispute an order and upload his/ her evidence. This feature can be used as data exfiltration channel.

item.title: Prisma Cloud Defenders
item.description:
Tl;dr - unfixed information disclosure in Prisma Cloud defenders

This post is about how to abuse a agent of a cloud security solution to get information which you should not know, like what security controls are applied, what assets the victim owns and the owners of the assets.

Introduction

A few months ago I was examining the Prisma Cloud configuration of my workplace and accidentally discovered an information disclosure issue of Prisma Cloud defender (the agent). This issue has been reported to Palo Alto as security disclosure, however Palo Alto declared that this is an expected behavior.
